description: Pentacad CAM strategy registry — 12 strategies covering 2D, 2.5D, and the first wave of 3D finishing. Each entry is a stable contract: a CAM widget consumes the params, the post-processor consumes the toolpath, the simulator plays it back. Flow is the 13th strategy reserved for Phase 2.

strategies.name: 2D Contour
strategies.description: Cuts along a 2D profile at a constant Z. Inside / outside / on-line offset · per-pass step-down · lead-in / lead-out · tool comp G41/G42.

strategies.name: 2D Pocket
strategies.description: Clears the area inside a closed 2D boundary. Spiral / zig-zag / parallel · ramp / plunge entry · island avoidance.

strategies.name: Drill
strategies.description: Canned drilling cycles — peck (G83) · simple (G81) · breakthrough (G82) · tapping (G84).

strategies.name: Face
strategies.description: Top-face stock removal. Linear / spiral / 90° / 45° · leaves a flat reference surface for downstream ops.

strategies.name: Adaptive Clearing
strategies.description: High-speed roughing — keeps a constant tool-engagement angle, uses trochoidal motion, supports deeper axial step-downs at higher feeds.

strategies.name: Chamfer
strategies.description: Edge-break / chamfer along a 2D contour with a vee or bullnose tool. Width · angle · clear-pass.

strategies.name: Bore
strategies.description: Helical or rigid boring of a circular hole. Concentric ramp · final cleanup pass · spring-pass option.

strategies.name: Thread Mill
strategies.description: Thread-milling helix — internal / external · metric / unified · single-point or full-form tool.

strategies.name: 3D Parallel
strategies.description: 3D finishing — parallel scallop lines projected onto a surface at a fixed angle. Stepover-controlled scallop height.

strategies.name: 3D Radial
strategies.description: 3D finishing — radial passes around a centre point. Ideal for round bosses / pockets where parallel would leave starvation marks.

strategies.name: 3D Scallop
strategies.description: Constant-scallop-height finishing — toolpath follows the surface curvature, keeping cusp height uniform regardless of slope.

strategies.name: 3D Projection
strategies.description: Projects a 2D pattern (curve / drawing) onto a 3D surface — engraving, decorative finishing, reference scribing.

deferred.name: 3D Flow
deferred.reason: Reserved for Phase 2 — flow-line finishing follows surface u/v parameter lines and needs a parametric surface representation Pentacad doesn't ship until OCCT WASM lands.
